name: Bow of Arjuna
description: The Bow of Arjuna, also known as the Gandiva, is a celestial bow of immense power and significance in Hindu mythology, particularly within the epic Mahabharata. Created by Brahma and originally wielded by Prajapati, Indra, and Soma, it was eventually given to Agni, the god of fire. Agni, in turn, presented it to Arjuna, the Pandava prince renowned for his archery skills, as a gift during the burning of the Khandava forest. The Gandiva is described as an unbreakable bow, capable of launching arrows with unparalleled speed, accuracy, and force. It is said to possess the ability to never miss its target and to generate divine arrows with unique and devastating effects. The bow played a pivotal role in Arjuna's victories throughout the Kurukshetra War, enabling him to vanquish formidable opponents such as Karna and Bhishma. Its strength was so great that only Arjuna was deemed worthy to wield it. After the war, Arjuna was instructed to return the Gandiva to Varuna, the god of the ocean, marking the end of its earthly service and its return to the celestial realm. The Gandiva symbolizes Arjuna's unwavering dedication to dharma, his exceptional skill as an archer, and the divine grace bestowed upon him.
subtitle: The Celestial Bow of Unmatched Power
origin: Created by Brahma, passed down through deities, gifted to Arjuna by Agni.
currentLocation: Returned to Varuna in the celestial ocean.

keyMyths.title: Acquisition of Gandiva
keyMyths.description: Arjuna receives the Gandiva from Agni during the burning of the Khandava forest as a reward for assisting the fire god in consuming the forest despite Indra's attempts to stop him. This event marks the beginning of Arjuna's legendary archery prowess.

keyMyths.title: The Kurukshetra War
keyMyths.description: The Gandiva plays a crucial role in the Kurukshetra War, enabling Arjuna to defeat numerous powerful warriors and ultimately contribute to the Pandavas' victory against the Kauravas. Its divine power makes Arjuna a formidable opponent on the battlefield.

sources.author: Vyasa
sources.description: The primary source for the story of Arjuna and the Gandiva.
sources.text: The primary source for the story of Arjuna and the Gandiva.

sources.author: Wendy Doniger
